specification.topic-statement: The escape sequence parser translates raw ANSI CSI byte sequences received from the terminal into semantic input actions, recognising arrow keys, mouse scroll events, function keys, and keyboard shortcuts.

scope.in-scope: Recognition of plain arrow keys, modifier-qualified arrow keys (Ctrl), mouse SGR scroll events, CSI u encoded keys, tilde-terminated function keys, and Home/End keys; the escape byte buffer and overflow handling.
scope.boundary: The main input parser routes raw bytes to this module once an ESC byte has been seen. Initial ESC detection and UTF-8 decoding are handled by the caller.
Shifted-key codepoint translation for CSI u sequences is delegated to the keyboard layout translator. This module passes through the result.

field.name: capacity
field.note: 16 bytes; buffer overflow resets state and emits UNKNOWN

field.name: position
field.note: zero-based byte count of currently buffered bytes

field.name: type
field.note: one of the semantic action identifiers below

behavior.buffer-management:
Each byte of the escape sequence is appended to a 16-byte circular buffer. If the buffer reaches 16 bytes and the sequence has not been resolved, the state is reset and UNKNOWN is emitted. This prevents the parser from becoming stuck on unrecognised or malformed sequences.
On any resolved or unresolvable sequence the buffer position and in-escape flag are reset before returning.
behavior.plain-arrow-keys:
Two-byte sequences of the form ESC [ followed by A, B, C, or D are recognised as arrow keys.
- A → ARROW_UP - B → ARROW_DOWN - C → ARROW_RIGHT - D → ARROW_LEFT
behavior.modified-arrow-keys:
Five-byte sequences of the form ESC [ 1 ; M followed by A, B, C, or D are recognised as modifier-qualified arrow keys. The modifier byte M is masked with ~128 to remove the NumLock bit.
Modifier value 5 (Ctrl) maps the arrow to navigation actions: - Up → NAV_PARENT (navigate to parent agent) - Down → NAV_CHILD (navigate to most recent child agent) - Left → NAV_PREV_SIBLING - Right → NAV_NEXT_SIBLING
Modifier value 1 (no modifier) maps to the plain arrow actions. Other modifier values emit UNKNOWN.
behavior.mouse-sgr-scroll:
Sequences of the form ESC [ < ... M or ESC [ < ... m are the SGR mouse protocol. The button code is extracted from the numeric field between the `<` character and the first `;`.
- Button 64 → SCROLL_UP - Button 65 → SCROLL_DOWN - All other button codes → UNKNOWN
Both M (button press) and m (button release) terminators are accepted for the scroll actions.
behavior.csi-u-encoded-keys:
Sequences ending with `u` of the form ESC [ keycode ; modifiers u are the CSI u extended keyboard protocol. The keycode and modifier are extracted as decimal integers. The modifier is masked with ~128 to remove the NumLock bit.
Keycodes greater than 50000 are rejected as UNKNOWN.
With modifier value 1 (no modifier): - Keycode 13 → NEWLINE - Keycode 9 → TAB - Keycode 127 → BACKSPACE - Keycode 27 → ESCAPE - Keycodes 32–126 (printable ASCII) → CHAR with that codepoint - Keycodes 128–1114111 (Unicode range) → CHAR with that codepoint - All other keycodes → UNKNOWN
With modifier value 2 (Shift): - Keycodes 32–126 → CHAR with the shifted codepoint produced by the keyboard layout translator; if translation fails, the original codepoint is used
With modifier value 5 (Ctrl): - Keycode 99 (c) → CTRL_C - Keycode 97 (a) → CTRL_A - Keycode 101 (e) → CTRL_E - Keycode 107 (k) → CTRL_K - Keycode 110 (n) → CTRL_N - Keycode 112 (p) → CTRL_P - Keycode 117 (u) → CTRL_U - Keycode 119 (w) → CTRL_W - All other keycodes → UNKNOWN
With modifier value other than 1, 2, or 5: - Any keycode with modifier 13 (Enter with modifier) → INSERT_NEWLINE
Other modifier values produce UNKNOWN.
behavior.tilde-function-keys:
Sequences ending with `~` are tilde-terminated function keys. A single numeric key code precedes the tilde.
- Code 1 → CTRL_A (Home) - Code 3 → DELETE (forward delete) - Code 4 → CTRL_E (End) - Code 5 → PAGE_UP - Code 6 → PAGE_DOWN - All other codes → UNKNOWN
behavior.home-end-keys:
Sequences of the form ESC [ H or ESC [ F: - H → CTRL_A (line start) - F → CTRL_E (line end)
Modified forms ESC [ 1 ; M H and ESC [ 1 ; M F are also recognised; the modifier value is validated but not used — H and F always produce CTRL_A and CTRL_E respectively.
behavior.discardable-sequences:
Some CSI sequences are recognised as known-uninteresting and silently discarded: - Byte `m` when one or more bytes are buffered (SGR colour/formatting) - Three-byte sequences ending with `~` for unknown function key codes
These emit UNKNOWN without further processing.
Modifier bytes in modified arrow keys and CSI u sequences are always masked with ~128 before comparison. This normalises input across terminals that set the high bit to indicate NumLock state.
